title: US president Donald Trump arrives in Scotland for four-day visit

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Trump says 'great to be in Scotland', as he lands for four-day trip US president Donald Trump said "it's great to be in Scotland" as he landed for a four-day private visit. After Air Force One touched down at Prestwick Airport, just before 20:30, the US president was met by Scottish Secretary Ian Murray and Warren Stephens, US Ambassador to the UK. Trump spoke to journalists before the presidential motorcade left for his Turnberry resort, in South Ayrshire, where he is expected to play golf on Saturday.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Speaking about Sir Keir Starmer, who he is due to meet on Monday, he said: "I like your prime minister. He's slightly more liberal than I am - as you probably heard - but he's a good man. He got a trade deal done ." Trump added: "You know, they've been working on this deal for 12 years, he got it done - that's a good deal, it's a good deal for the UK."

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: The president earlier also described Scotland's First Minister John Swinney as "a good man" and said he was looking forward to meeting him. Swinney has pledged to "essentially speak out for Scotland".

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Planespotters gather to catch glimpse of Air Force One
